items.question: What is the Sitecore XM Cloud Starter Kit?
items.answer: A primarily Next.js, based reference implementation for Sitecore XM Cloud that accelerates development using modern tooling and best practices.

items.question: Which frameworks are supported?
items.answer: The starter kit is optimized for Next.js App Router and Sitecore Content SDK, with full support for SSR, SSG, and server components.

items.question: What starter applications are included in this repository?
items.answer: The repository includes basic-nextjs (simple XM Cloud integration), kit-nextjs-article-starter (Solterra & Co., editorial/lifestyle), kit-nextjs-location-finder (Alaris, car brand with location finder), kit-nextjs-product-listing (SYNC, product/audio gear), kit-nextjs-skate-park (component demos), and basic-spa (Angular SPA with Node proxy). Each lives under /examples and can be developed and deployed independently.

items.question: How do i use the starter kits in XMCloud?
items.answer: When you setup a new project in XMCLoud select `Use starter kit` or select `Use your own code` and link to your own fork of the starter kits repository

items.question: What practices do the starters demonstrate?
items.answer: Starters demonstrate Tailwind-based styling with BYOC (Bring Your Own Componenet), modular component architecture with variants, content-first and field-driven rendering with XM Cloud, GEO and AEO, localization (en and en-CA), safe datasource destructuring and fallbacks, editing and preview mode handling, error boundaries, Automated GIT Workflow with Unit Tests, and TypeScript-first development with strict types and proper interfaces.

items.question: How do I run a starter locally?
items.answer: Navigate to the project, select the target environment (e.g., dev), open Developer Settings, and copy the sample .env from Local Development into .env.local in the example folder (e.g., examples/basic-nextjs). Enable Preview, then run npm install and npm run dev to start the app at http://localhost:3000.

items.question: What does xmcloud.build.json configure?
items.answer: xmcloud.build.json is the main deployment config for XM Cloud. It defines renderingHosts (path, nodeVersion, jssDeploymentSecret, enabled, buildCommand, runCommand, postActions), and authoringPath. Each starter can be enabled or disabled for deployment; the file drives build and deployment in XM Cloud.

items.question: How do I contribute to the project?
items.answer: Create a fork of the starters repository; Feature branches are created from main in your own fork; Pull requests target the dmz branch (not main) of Sitecore starters repository. Main stays deployable; dmz is where PRs are merged and validated. see .CONTRIBUTING.md for the full guide.

items.question: Does the starter kit support GEO (Generative Engine Optimization) and AEO (Answer Engine Optimization)?
items.answer: Yes. The starter kit includes semantic HTML, JSO-LD structured data, an LLM sitemap for AI discovery, AI-specific endpoints, explicit robots.txt allowances for GPTBot, ClaudeBot, PerplexityBot and other crawlers, and dynamic sitemap.xml linking. These features enable effective GEO and AEO behavior. See docs/AI_CRAWLER_ACCESS.md for configuration.

items.question: What Universal Coding Principles and best practices does the project demonstrate?
items.answer: The project follows Universal Standards: DRY (Don't Repeat Yourself, reusable utilities, composition over inheritance), Locality of Behavior Principle, SOLID (Single Responsibility, Open/Closed via composition, Dependency Inversion), and Code Clarity (self-documenting code, meaningful names, explicit behavior). It also applies modular design, unidirectional data flow, validation at boundaries, testable code with minimal dependencies, and quality assurance via code review and CI. See CLAUDE.md and .cursor/rules/general.mdc.
